title: Grades of Student

content.problemStatement: Write a program to print the grades of a student based on the marks they have obtained provided as input. The student is graded **A**  if marks are greater than **90**, **B** if marks are greater than **70**, **C** if greater than or equal to **40**, else **F**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer marks (0 ≤ marks ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single character representing the grade (A, B, C, or F) on its own line.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to compare the given marks with the fixed thresholds. The grading rule is: A if marks > 90, B if marks > 70, C if marks >= 40, otherwise F. A simple chain of if‑else statements yields the answer in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input and output buffers.
